required_edits: Fix the committed clotho-git-weaver closure or the git-weaver's actual imports: git.mjs imports deriveNodeId/validateEdgeInput from ../registry.mjs, and registry.mjs is claimed in WEAVER_IMPL_FILES as the ONLY dependency ({registry.mjs, git.mjs}) — but if clotho/registry.mjs itself contains any accepted literal relative import (it predates this PR and is not shown; the closure test would catch a mismatch only if registry.mjs is genuinely leaf), the committed inventory is wrong. More critically: provide clotho/registry.mjs in the review artifact or its derived closure evidence; the review packet omits the bytes of a file that is a member of BOTH committed closures, so faithfulness of the committed inventories cannot be verified from the artifact as presented. If registry.mjs is leaf, attach that evidence; the committed inventory equality claim currently rests solely on the self-referential test run.

test-closure.mjs section 9 (AM-34 required test 19) proves single-definition and that code.mjs imports resolveRelativeSpecifier, but it does NOT prove the closure derivation and the (future) advisory scanner import the same exported classifier AND resolver as the task text requires — the closure-derivation side is proven only implicitly (deriveAcceptedClosure lives in the same file as the classifier/resolver and calls them lexically, unverified by the test). Add an explicit assertion that deriveAcceptedClosure's implementation consumes classifyModuleLoads and resolveRelativeSpecifier (e.g. lexical assertion over util.mjs source that deriveAcceptedClosure's body calls both by name), so the shared-consumer proof does not silently regress when Task 5 lands the scanner.

makeCountedSource undercounts on early return/break: test-util section 1 asserts observed_count === 1 after breaking at 'b' — but the consumer DID complete consumption of 'a' AND received 'b'. Per D26 'inspected means opened, read, and processed to edge-extraction eligibility without fatal error', an item the consumer received but abandoned via break is correctly not counted — this matches the spec — however the generator's `observed++` placement also fails to count the FINAL item until the iterator is resumed past it, meaning a consumer that reads the last item, fully processes it, and then breaks (never triggering the final next()) records observed_count = N-1 with exhausted=false even though every source was fully processed. For the git/code weavers using for...of to completion this is invisible, but the accounting semantics diverge from D26's definition of 'inspected' for the last element under any consumer that stops after the final yield. Either document this as the frozen driver contract (weavers MUST run iterators to natural completion — which D29 requires anyway) with a unit pinning the N-1 behavior as intended, or restructure so completion of the final item is observable. At minimum add the pinning unit so Task 4b/5 drivers don't misread the boundary.

code.mjs emits `unrepresentable-consumer` for any import that resolves outside the seeded file set (`!fileBlob.has(r.repoRelative)`), not only for specifiers that fail to resolve to a real file below the closed roots. A relative import that resolves to a REAL existing file which happens to be outside the closed package roots (or a non-.mjs real file, kind 'ambiguous-extension') is warned as unrepresentable-consumer even though the spec says unrepresentable-consumer is ONLY for 'an import whose specifier does not resolve to a real file below the closed roots'. The conflation of resolver-failure kinds (symlink, escape, ambiguous-extension) with not-seeded into one warning is defensible for symlink/escape but the warning text asserts '(no seeded file below closed roots)' for ALL cases including resolvable-but-excluded files; add a fixture distinguishing a real file outside closed roots and confirm/adjust intended semantics so the warning matches its stated condition.

test-code.mjs never asserts the file-level fallback endpoint carrying repository_ref and blob_sha in the locator shape assertions demanded by the task ('file-level fallback endpoints and locators including repository_ref and blob_sha'): it checks node-id equality (which transitively binds locators) but never directly asserts e.from_locator.locator.repository_ref/blob_sha for the repository-file -> repository-file case as test-git.mjs does for its edges. Add the direct locator-field assertions for at least one file-level edge and one symbol-level edge in test-code.mjs.
hard_stops: Unverifiable committed-closure claim: clotho/registry.mjs is a member of both committed WEAVER_IMPL_FILES closures but its source is absent from the review artifact; the equality proof (test-closure section 1) executes against the real tree, but this council seat cannot independently confirm from the artifact that registry.mjs has no further accepted relative imports (which would make BOTH committed inventories incomplete and the test's pass contingent on bytes not under review). Faithfulness review of the central Task 4a deliverable — the committed per-weaver inventories — requires the closure-member bytes or their derived-closure evidence in the packet.
rationale: The implementation is high quality and close to faithful. Strong points: the frozen REQUIRED_INVENTORY_IDS table matches the normative table exactly with a real equality unit (test-inventory §2); LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS matches D32 exactly with deep-equal, exact-key/value, deep-freeze, and mutation units (§3); PACKAGE_ROOTS/DOC_ROOTS/CONTRACT_FILES/RUN_SOURCES/exclusions are committed constants with existence checks (D17); only git+code inventories are committed per AM-17; the D33 classifier/resolver is single-sourced in util.mjs with a genuine comment/string/regex/template-aware lexer, and the closure test covers every accepted form (including export * as ns), recursion, cycles, unreachable dynamic import, permitted merkle-dag traversal, and all fatal modes with junction-based symlink tests that fail rather than skip; the counted iterator hides accounting from the weaver (D26/D29 driver-held accessor); physicalContainment lstats every component, rejects symlinked roots without following, and resolves only the deepest safe ancestor (D21); validateGitArgs freezes the exact two log shapes plus rev-parse/rev-list/hash-object with order/duplication/extra-arg rejection and unoverridable spawn options; git.mjs emits exactly the two frozen log invocations with first-result selection, full-SHA validation fatal on malformed output, warn-no-edge on empty; code.mjs threads all four endpoint shapes with alias resolution, no-export preservation as repository-file->code-symbol, dedup, unused-import suppression, and determinism units. However: (1) the review packet omits clotho/registry.mjs, a member of both committed closures — I cannot stake a signature on inventory-closure equality when a closure member's bytes are outside the artifact (hard stop, resolvable by supplying the file or its evidence); (2) AM-34 test 19 is only half-proven — the shared-consumer assertion covers code.mjs but the closure derivation's own consumption of the shared classifier/resolver is asserted nowhere; (3) the counted iterator's last-item accounting boundary diverges from D26's 'inspected' definition under a stop-after-final-yield consumer and deserves a pinning unit before 4b builds the driver on it; (4) unrepresentable-consumer fires for resolvable-but-not-seeded files, wider than the frozen 'does not resolve to a real file below the closed roots' condition, with a warning message asserting a condition that may be false; (5) test-code lacks the direct locator-field (repository_ref/blob_sha) assertions the task explicitly enumerates for file-level fallback endpoints. None of these look like scope expansion — they are faithfulness gaps and one evidentiary gap. Revise, not reject: the architecture matches v12 Task 4a as written and the fixes are narrow.
